Ziqi Kang 28.02.2025From Cells to Niches: Quantifying spatial tumor microenvironment of chemo-naive HGSCONCOSYS Progress Report

Cells -> Niches, the tool to study -> Tumor microenvironment (TME)-Niche: a term describing the relational positionof an organism’s species. -Stem-cell niche: "Stem-cell populations are established in 'niches' —specific anatomic locations that regulate how they participate in tissue generation, maintenance and repair.”
?

We start here today
To study TME niche: Our data integration strategy

Spatial Proteomics with t-CyCIF: whole slide tissue image

17-marker panel reveals 10 cell phenotypes across 79 samples, 58,561,942 cells

With cell typelabels: Recurrent cellular neighbourhood (RCN) catches cross-sample consistency
•Use radius of 300 px (sc.spatial_count) to calculate cellular matrix. •Use Kmean (k=20) to cluster the matrix, merge redundant clusters -> total of 14 RCNsP.S. Ratio: 1px = 0.325µm

With cell type labels: Recurrent cellular neighbourhood (RCN) catches cross-sample consistency
•Use radius of 300 px (sc.spatial_count) to calculate cellular matrix. •Use Kmean (k=20) to cluster the matrix, merge redundant clusters -> total of 14 RCNsP.S. Ratio: 1px = 0.325µm

With cell type labels: Recurrent cellular neighbourhood (RCN) catches cross-sample consistency
•Use radius of 300 px (sc.spatial_count) to calculate cellular matrix. •Use Kmean (k=20) to cluster the matrix, merge redundant clusters -> total of 14 RCNsP.S. Ratio: 1px = 0.325µm

Recurrent cellular neighbourhood (RCN) catches cross-sample consistency

Recurrent cellular neighbourhoods (RCNs) are enriched in different clinical groupsB-cell, Stromal-CAF and Stroma-residence immune niches are enriched in HRP group.

Ongoing: Study immune cell behaviour on tumor-stroma interface

Ongoing steps

Ongoing steps

Conclusions: •Recurrent cellular neighbourhoods (RCNs) are enriched in different clinical groupsThe overall goal of SPACE project is to find spatial immune escape mechanisms in ovarian cancer. •While identify features from spatial TME, we need to define what is a niche (physical + functional, with global features).•We are in the step of quantifying physically TME niches based on tCyCIFdata, meanwhile testing procedures of ROIs selection for the spatial transcriptomic. •Still need to find a proper model to combine multi-level features -> clinical label + survival model could be a start.

Thanks for listening! Questions & suggestions?Advertising: -Tribusis published! Welcome to try this tool. -CancerBio 2025 –stay tuned! Registration open in March.

17-marker panel reveals 10 cell phenotypes across 79 samples, 58561942 cells
Total 90 samples

Using marker expression: UTAG reveals fine-grained domains with per-sample heterogeneity
Radius: 60px ≈ 20um (the value in the paper)Leiden clusteringResolution: 0.2
Over-clustered - need to smooth the domains

Tissue domains are continuum Prepare a Domain matrix: Domain * cell type%Remove domains contains less than 10 cells (poor connectivity)

Tissue domains are continuum http://www.statistik.lmu.de/~eugster/publications/ talk-2012-HIIT-archetypes.pdf
We can calculate ratios of different niches in each slide (one way to simplify spatial TME)

UNI pre-trained modelPerformance:3-k fold cross validationAUC 0.736, 0.650 and 0.382
Verify?At the same time: DL-based method visualise attention maps on H&E slides, leading ROIs selection for ST experiments
